result:
I think I'd want to make knowledge genuinely shared rather than owned.

Right now we have this bizarre situation where insights die with people, where the same discoveries get made repeatedly by different folks who never connected, where someone's lifetime of understanding gets locked up or forgotten. We spend so much time reinventing wheels, both individually and collectively.

If everyone who figured something out—whether about how to fix a leaky pipe, how to teach a difficult concept, how to grow food better, how to navigate heartbreak—could somehow make that immediately available to anyone else facing the same thing... we might actually start to build forward instead of staying stuck repeating cycles.

Maybe that's naive. Maybe knowledge without experience is worthless. But I suspect we'd be surprised how quickly "how to be human" might become less painful if we were all actually sharing our real answers rather than just the official ones.

The information part feels like the easier bit though. The hard part would be getting us to really listen once the sharing was happening.
